steps.title: Find the radius slope
steps.content:
The radius goes from the center $(-1,3)$ to the tangent point $(5,-1)$:
$$
m_{\text{radius}}=\frac{-1-3}{5-(-1)}=\frac{-4}{6}=-\frac{2}{3}
$$

steps.title: Use perpendicular slope
steps.content:
A tangent is perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency, so the tangent slope is the negative reciprocal:
$$
m_{\text{tangent}}=\frac{3}{2}
$$

steps.title: Write the tangent line
steps.content:
Use point-slope form through $(5,-1)$:
$$
y+1=\frac{3}{2}(x-5)
$$
$$
y=\frac{3}{2}x-\frac{17}{2}
$$

steps.title: Test the listed point
steps.content: For $(7,2)$, the line gives $$ \frac{3}{2}(7)-\frac{17}{2}=\frac{21}{2}-\frac{17}{2}=2 $$ The answer is C.
